channel.title: Comments for page "Audio Adaptations"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Audio Adaptations"

item.title: Re: New artwork
item.content:encoded:
This has now been updated (updated a few weeks ago actually), thank you.

On this wiki uploading image files to the page is restricted to the page's creator and moderators. So asking like this on the discussion page like you did is the way to do it.

item.title: Re: Series list errors
item.content:encoded: Thanks for bringing that to our attention! TSATPWTCOTTTADC has just addressed the matter.

item.title: Series list errors
item.content:encoded: all the links to series 1 to 5 (not behind the How to Add collapsible) link to series 6 audio page.

item.title: New artwork
item.content:encoded: How do we go about updating the artwork displayed in the Producer list? The SCP Foundation Database cover art has since changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love what you've done with the place, TSAT! This rocks!

item.title: Re: Free content only?
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'd leave those off.

item.title: Free content only?
item.content:encoded: I have a number of Joke SCP readings I could add, but they are all Patreon-exclusive and so are behind a paywall. Should those be left off of this list?

item.title: Re: Character Limit
item.content:encoded: Thanks. And yes that is possible, I should have mentioned that. Having the full name in that list at the bottom does not pose any issues. As an example, because of the character limit requirement another content producer is called Audio Archive throughout most of this page, but SCP Foundation Audio Archive ("Audio Archive") in the list of producers at the bottom.

item.title: Re: Character Limit
item.content:encoded:
"SCPDataPodcast" would work. I use it for my website, email, twitter, etc., so it should be recognizable.

EDIT: Would it be possible to keep one instance of the full name somewhere (like in the Producers' Hub) with SCP Data Podcast next to it in parentheses? If not, that's fine.

item.title: Re: Character Limit
item.content:encoded:
Zyn reached out to staff responsible for this page's upkeep and this suggestion of yours seems like it'd turn this resource page into the most useful version of itself:

do we all just need to be much more selective about what's added?

We'll try a 3 readings per article maximum. We'll prune the stuff we have already (Page revision 506) to adhere to that limit and will add a notice to the top of the page that anyone is free to add past that limit, but staff will occasionally prune this page and will again whittle things down to the limit, keeping the adaptations that offer the best experience. The selection is up to staff discretion, but the it will be affected by aspects of the adaptation such as audio quality, completeness and adherence to the source material (as opposed to making new things up to fill in redactions for example), and presence of proper author credit and licensing disclosures.

ETA: The full name of your podcast is causing issues on mobile due to its length. We sometimes have to ask content creators to pick a shorter name for use on this list. Something under 20 characters in length. What would you like yours to be?

item.title: Re: Character Limit
item.content:encoded: Good to know. Thank you!

item.title: Re: Character Limit
item.content:encoded:
Also, if this isn't the place for this question, let me know.

This is the right place for the question, though the Proposals and Policy subforum would have offered better visibility. Also, typically for questions directed at staff, try to message an admin or mod so they can notify others.

I will bring this to our MAST and tech team leads.

item.title: Character Limit
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I'm new. I went in to update the name of my podcast adaptation (which has changed since someone added it last year), and to add the rest of my catalog. I finished and went to save, but it told me the 200000 character limit for the page source had been reached. One by one I removed the additions I made, but even after I definitely got it well below 200000, it's still telling me the same thing. My changes are currently saved as a draft, but I can throw it out if necessary.

Does anyone know if there are any plans to break the article up into multiple pages considering how long it's gotten, or do we all just need to be much more selective about what's added? Even if we are more selective, the page is still extremely close to the character limit, apparently.

Also, if this isn't the place for this question, let me know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, can we add adaptations but the articles have been translated into another language?

item.title: Re: Another Question
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for spending the time to help keep this page up date.

The audio quality of live streams like the one you linked is often lower than other readings one can find, or they are interrupted by off-topic talk.

At this point, as this collection of links has grown to be really large, to make sure that this page remains a high quality resource for readers/listeners, the contributor (you, in this case) can start to exercise discretion on which readings to add and which to forego.

For example, the hundreds of readings available from the many SCP text-to-speech robotic voice reading channels on YouTube are not included here due to their almost universal low quality when compared to human readers (The channel ReadOut being a notably high quality exception).

If you wish to include readings from a live stream archive and have the patience to find and link the right timestamps for the readings in it, then that's fine - go ahead.

That YouTube link in your post didn't work for me for some reason. In case it doesn't work for anyone else, here it is in a different format - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uRfXUVCeq3E.

item.title: Another Question
item.content:encoded:
Should livestreams like this be placed here? While they do read out the SCP the reading itself is only a portion of the content.

Also, I think I'll take it upon myself to keep this whole thing fairly up to date. :)

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your response! :)

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: Looks solid to me.

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to just remove the "Latest Additions to This Catalog" section for now so you don't have to enter the same information twice.
If you upload a lot, your recent additions to that section will soon need to be removed to prevent it getting too bloated. I see needing to enter text that quickly gets removed anyway as a waste of effort.

item.title: Question
item.content:encoded: I'd like to inquire about how exactly the "Latest Additions to This Catalog" section is handled. I upload my readings daily in numerical order and I think that'd quickly bloat that section and considering there are readings from 2014 there I'm kind of confused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking I'd add the newly minted FAM Radio Hub page to a list here, but now I'm not 100% sure it fits, since it's a podcast, not a reading/voice actor?

Thoughts? Is there a page for podcasts/channels or should I just start a new section here? Please advise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff may remove content from this page as is deemed necessary, but yes, I believe it's open for additions. Please place any new entries in the proper sections/categories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is anyone allowed to put their own SCP readings onto the list? I have a few of my own that I'd like to add

item.title: Re: Massive Overhaul
item.content:encoded: Noting that Dexanote and myself gave permission for this update.

item.title: Re: Massive Overhaul
item.content:encoded: I was just thinking about this page today, so good timing. :D Looks nice! Table headers aren't working on the second and last collapsibles, though.

item.title: Massive Overhaul
item.content:encoded:
The Audio Adaptation Page is now squeaky clean! Check out our easy to navigate collapsibles, our organization by SCP Number for easy search (and alphabetization in the Tales/Misc), and our VA tab where we can find our favorite voice actors' on-site hubs and off-site channels!

Please please please keep it neat and tidy!

Edit for TL333s: Got it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't know changes to the format were currently being discussed when I made the latest edit. For anyone concerned, I grouped together readings by narrator and ordered the narrators by where their highest-positioned reading was previously in the list (in order to step on as few toes as possible).

Rather than tabs, or perhaps in combination, I would suggest that we have a table sortable by various categories such as SCP number, date, and narrator. In either case, it certainly needs to be possible to view readings by the same narrator grouped together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd be okay with tabs, but I'm not sure about others, especially mobile users. I'll nudge this over to some other staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How about we move it fully into the future…

TABS! (Edit: I'd also be down with Collapsibles)

Series 1
Series 2
Series 3
Series 4
Joke SCPs
Tales
Miscellaneous Hub Links

(Keep the current format of Title, Link, Article, and VA)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think alphabetical/SCP number organization would be a good idea. (Maybe put the SCPs first by number, then jokes/etc., then tales.) I should also probably stick my request thread into my entries, huh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm on board with an organization effort. I have two ideas: either alphabetically/by SCP-Number, or by VA. Also, we could add links to specific audio hubs at the bottom maybe?

Here's how I organize my audio hub for example:

http://www.scp-wiki.net/theesherm-s-great-scip-dictations

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey quick question:
As a frequent poster, my ocd is really starting to kick in. I'm trying to think of some way to organize these recordings better, maybe by author, or by type (SCP, tale, etc.), but I don't want to incur someone's wrath by changing things around without approval. Any thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, when did this happen and why did I never hear about it? :O

EDIT: I take requests (and should actually be able to fulfill them now!), and that thread is over here.

item.title: Re: Question!
item.content:encoded: Done.

item.title: Re: Question!
item.content:encoded: I feel like there should be, since this is a table. I'll nudge one of our technical team staff about this.

item.title: Question!
item.content:encoded: Hello! - I'm wondering now that we have other talented people creating audio adaptations, is there any way to label/group them in accordance to what VA created them? - (I don't want to change things and have people irritated that i'm editing the site).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is anyone allowed to make an audio adaptation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to ask. If I created an audio adaptation is it okay if I added it to the list? Or do they have to be approved by staff first?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like pages with no rating module at the top. Makes me work to upvote it, makes me feel special for upvoting. We need more audio adaptions. Hear, hear!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! More Volgun stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Volgun, really wonderful adaptations. You're intonation reminds me of John Cusack which in mind is perfect for these.

Brilliant!

Also Seth McFarlan, so I can imagine Brian reading these!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew TheVolgun was a member of the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If your audio adaptations are awesome… so can your writings! I'm pretty sure that there would be some people who would help you out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dude, if it's just spelling and wording, that's what we're here to help with, whether in the chat room or in the forums! We're all about ideas here and while, yes, good writing does count for a lot, I think most members recognize that everyone's gotta start somewhere.

If you'd rather not the sandbox draft be semi-public, you can always send a private message to folks who've offered their critique services. I'd be happy to help, for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someday … maybe. I'm dyslexic, so I think I would get ripped to pieces by the community if I did. hahaha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Volgun, you should write your own SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: added a couple more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am both inspired and discouraged to try by the quality of his audio logs. -_-

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have all my upvotes. All of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is there an audio log of 990-02 if it was taken during a dream?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With all of the readings Volgun is doing, I'm inspired to do some of my own… Hmm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That Volgun guy is pretty damn good.
